Aerospace Apr 7, 2026

Artemis 2 swings around the moon

Four astronauts from the United States and Canada became the humans to travel the furthest from the Earth April 6 as they went around the moon on the Artemis 2 mission.

Artemis 2's successful lunar flyby validates NASA's deep-space infrastructure for sustained human exploration beyond Earth orbit.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Apr 7, 2026

The Problem With Ultra Long-Haul Flights Airlines Don’t Talk About

Why Air New Zealand is unable to compete on its flagship route.

Fuel consumption and aircraft economics on ultra long-haul routes create structural disadvantages for carriers lacking scale or favorable fuel pricing agreements.

See Simple Flying →

Military Apr 7, 2026

5 Fighter Jets With The Most Advanced Radar Systems

The fighters with the longest striking range.

Advanced radar systems extend combat engagement ranges beyond 200 nautical miles, fundamentally reshaping air superiority tactics and interception capabilities.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Air Canada Expands To The Canary Islands & Latin America, Thanks To The Airbus A321XLR & A220

The carrier is making big moves in high-yield markets.

Air Canada's expansion into long-range leisure markets demonstrates how newer-generation aircraft like the A321XLR enable profitable transatlantic operations from smaller hubs without requiring widebody aircraft.

See Simple Flying →

PAI
Regulatory Apr 7, 2026

SMS Declaration of Compliance

What the SMS Declaration of Compliance means for operators and what the FAA expects.

The SMS Declaration of Compliance is now a mandatory filing requirement that demonstrates operators have implemented FAA-approved safety management systems, creating accountability through formal documentation.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Here's How Air Canada's Airbus A321XLR 1-1 Lie-Flat Suites Compare To Widebody Business Class

The A321XLR's 4,700-nautical-mile range opens secondary European cities but does the 12.2-foot-wide cabin match the widebody experience?

Air Canada's A321XLR single-aisle configuration sacrifices widebody cabin comfort for route flexibility, creating a mid-market business product that narrows rather than expands premium connectivity options.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

“There’s A Huge Demand”: The Boeing 787-8 Dreamliner Demolished After Only 13 Flying Hours

A barely flown Boeing 787-8 is scrapped because its parts are worth more than the whole.

Supply chain disruptions have made aircraft component values exceed airframe worth, incentivizing operators to scrap serviceable aircraft for parts rather than fly them.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

The Cost Of Flying Premium Economy In 2026 Compared To Economy

The hidden truth about premium economy.

Premium economy pricing gaps are widening as airlines leverage ancillary revenue strategies to offset narrowbody fleet economics in long-haul markets.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Apr 7, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS shifts aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating hazards before they become incidents.

See PAI Consulting — SMS Quick Takes →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

The World's Largest Airline By Number Of Hubs

American Airlines reigns supreme on this front.

American Airlines' hub-centric network model provides competitive advantages in connecting passengers and cargo despite higher operational complexity than point-to-point competitors.

See Simple Flying →

Drones Apr 6, 2026

China tests heavy cargo drone designed for extreme terrain

China has completed the maiden flight of a new 7-tonne autonomous cargo aircraft, taking a...

China's 7-tonne autonomous cargo drone represents a strategic capability to bypass infrastructure limitations in remote mountainous regions, potentially disrupting traditional air-cargo logistics in Asia-Pacific markets.

See sUAS News →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Not So Fast: How The Boeing 757 Made A Comeback At This Luxury Airline

The aircraft is surprisingly rejoining the airline's fleet.

The 757's resurgence at a luxury carrier suggests operators are finding renewed value in its long-range efficiency and cabin flexibility as newer aircraft face supply constraints.

See Simple Flying →

▶9:39
Regulatory Apr 7, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Risk Management

This video covers the safety management system (SMS) component safety risk management, which determines the need for, and adequacy of, new or revised risk controls based on the assessment of acceptable risk.

Small operators must establish formal risk assessment processes to justify control decisions, moving beyond intuitive safety practices to data-driven risk management.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Artemis II Crew Eclipses Record for Farthest Human Spaceflight

Editor’s note: This release was updated at 7:28 p.m. EDT on April 6, 2026, to reflect Orion’s final closest approach to the lunar surface from about 4,070 miles to about 4,067 miles. NASA also updated the farthest distance Orion will travel. Finally, NASA also made changes at 10 p.m. to the...

Artemis II's lunar trajectory demonstrates advanced navigation precision that will inform future deep-space mission planning and crew safety protocols for extended-duration flights beyond Earth orbit.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Downsizing: Allegiant Air To Close Bellingham & Savannah Crew Bases In November

As Allegiant Air prepares to close its Bellingham and Savannah crew bases, questions arise about the future of smaller airport hubs.

Allegiant's base closures signal the airline's shift away from secondary markets, potentially reshaping ultra-low-cost carrier strategy and competition at smaller regional airports.

See Simple Flying →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

NASA’s Northrop Grumman CRS-24 Mission Overview

NASA’s Northrop Grumman Commercial Resupply Services 24 mission, or Northrop Grumman CRS-24, will deliver approximately 11,000 pounds of science and supplies to the International Space Station. This mission will be the second flight of the Cygnus XL, the larger, more cargo-capable version of the...

The Cygnus XL's increased 11,000-pound payload capacity represents a significant operational upgrade for ISS resupply missions, reducing launch frequency requirements and costs for NASA's cargo operations.

See NASA →

▶20:47
Safety Apr 7, 2026

FAA Part 5 SMS for Small Operators: Safety Policy & Safety Promotion

This video provides an overview of implementing a safety management system (SMS), specifically aligned with FAA Part 5 regulations. Join us as we explore Blue Skies Charter, a fictional company, and learn about general SMS requirements, safety policy development and safety promotion strategies. This video covers essential components like leadership commitment, gap analysis, safety objectives, emergency response planning and employee training. Whether you're a small charter operator or interested in aviation safety, this video offers valuable insights into building an effective SMS.

Small charter operators can leverage structured SMS implementation to transform safety culture through documented policies, leadership commitment, and systematic training rather than reactive compliance.

See NBAA (YouTube) →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

K-State Salina Launches High School Aviation Camp

Weeklong program will include simulator work, ground school and up to three hours of flight time.

Hands-on flight experience during formative years creates pipeline talent for regional carriers facing pilot shortage challenges.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Moog Highlights Growing Satellite Bus Capabilities with Full‑Scale METEOR Reveal at Space Symposium

East Aurora, NY — Moog Inc. (NYSE: MOG.A and MOG.B), a worldwide designer, manufacturer and systems integrator of high-performance precision motion and fluid controls and control systems will highlight its […]...

Moog's METEOR satellite bus platform expands aerospace suppliers' vertical integration into spacecraft manufacturing, potentially reshaping competitive dynamics in commercial space.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

FOSSA targets Japan’s defense market as larger smallsats expand capabilities

Spanish startup FOSSA Systems is pushing into Japan’s defense market after securing a local partner to expand its reach, building on a shift beyond tiny picosatellites used to connect low-power monitoring devices toward more capable spacecraft for broader government applications.

FOSSA's expansion into Japan's defense sector signals that commercial smallsat operators are transitioning from IoT connectivity to mission-critical government applications, creating new revenue streams beyond consumer markets.

See SpaceNews →

Industry Apr 7, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Optimizing existing operations through technology integration offers near-term sustainability gains while aviation awaits breakthrough propulsion alternatives.

See MDPI →

Corporate Apr 6, 2026

Air Tractor Holdings Acquires Thrush Aircraft

Deal combines two ag aviation manufacturers under shared ownership.

Agricultural aviation consolidation reduces the competitive landscape, potentially affecting pricing and innovation in the specialized aerial application market.

See AVweb →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Hartzell Expands Top Prop Program

Update adds 150+ models, pricing changes for GA aircraft upgrades.

Hartzell's expanded propeller program now covers 150+ additional GA models, signaling increased retrofit market opportunities and competitive modernization options for fleet operators.

See AVweb →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

The Main Features That Made The Boeing 777 A Long-Haul Leader

Why the Boeing 777 is the most successful widebody airliner of all time.

The 777's twin-engine efficiency and composite materials reduced operating costs by 20% versus quad-engine competitors, fundamentally reshaping long-haul route economics.

See Simple Flying →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Poll: Will Phoenix Trigger Broader Landing Fees?

Could Phoenix-area landing fees spark broader GA change?

Phoenix's landing fee implementation may establish a precedent that pressures other cash-strapped airports to adopt similar revenue-generating policies, potentially reshaping GA economics nationwide.

See AVweb →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

Landing Fees and Flight Training in Phoenix

As Falcon Field and Mesa Gateway add landing fees for light aircraft, officials and flight schools describe different futures for the Phoenix area.

Phoenix-area flight schools face operational cost pressures from new landing fees at regional airports, potentially reshaping pilot training economics and location decisions across the basin.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Night Sky Network Celebrates Artemis II

Today, the crew of Artemis II reaches a milestone, traveling farther than any humans in recorded history, as they orbit the far side of the Moon, roughly 4,000 miles above the lunar surface. You can watch this historic broadcast on NASA’s YouTube channel today, April 6, 2026, at 10 AM Pacific (1 PM...

Artemis II's lunar orbit milestone demonstrates NASA's operational readiness for sustained deep-space human missions, validating long-duration life support and navigation systems critical for future Mars exploration programs.

See NASA →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

Grants

Grants Status Requests To submit a request, visit NASA General Information Request Form and complete the form. You will receive an automated email with the most commonly requested grant status information. Important Instructions: How to Fill Out the Form: Memorandum for NASA Grantee Community...

NASA's streamlined grant status inquiry process uses automated responses to handle routine requests, reducing administrative burden on both applicants and agency staff.

See NASA →

Military Apr 6, 2026

Lady of Larissa Flies Again: Incredible New Footage Emerges of the Secretive RQ-180 Spy Drone

New videos give an even clearer view – unquestionably our best ever – of the legendary classified stealth drone, previously seen landing in Greece last month. Last month, we reported on images taken in Larissa, Greece, showing what we, and others, concluded – based on prior sightings and comment...

Recent high-definition footage of the RQ-180 stealth drone provides the most detailed visual evidence yet of this classified platform's operational capabilities and design characteristics.

See The Aviationist →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

The Near Side of the Moon

The astronauts aboard NASA’s Orion spacecraft captured this photo of the Moon’s near side on April 4, 2026. The image features dark patches at center and right; these are ancient lava flows, which are unique to this side of the Moon. The Artemis II astronauts – astronauts Victor Glover, Reid...

Artemis II's lunar imagery validates navigation and imaging systems critical for sustained crewed missions beyond Earth orbit.

See NASA →

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Amazon Leo to serve as launchpad for more Delta brand experiences

The proverbial ‘gravitational pull’ of low-latency Low Earth Orbit (LEO) satellite-powered inflight connectivity is continuing unabated in commercial aviation, as Delta Air Lines earmarks Amazon Leo for an initial 500 tails from 2028. Announced on 31 March, the arrangement springboards off the US...

Delta's commitment to Amazon's Leo satellite constellation signals a shift toward LEO-based connectivity as airlines prioritize low-latency performance over traditional solutions, reshaping competitive inflight experience investments.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Apr 6, 2026

USAF F-16s Flying With AGM-158 JASSMs in Operation Epic Fury Against Iran

Long-range JASSM strikes still continue on Iranian targets, particularly against missile and drone production facilities. U.S. Air Force F-16s currently deployed in support of Operation Epic Fury have been shown in official photos carrying the AGM-158 Joint-Air-to-Surface Standoff Missile (JASSM)...

JASSM deployment in Operation Epic Fury demonstrates U.S. commitment to sustained standoff strike capability against distributed Iranian military-industrial targets across extended ranges.

See The Aviationist →

General Aviation Apr 6, 2026

National STOL Announces 2026 Competition Schedule

Series plans eight events across the U.S. beginning in April.

The STOL competition's expanded eight-event calendar signals growing market interest in short-takeoff-and-landing aircraft capabilities for operators seeking reduced-infrastructure alternatives.

See AVweb →

Drones Apr 6, 2026

Invisible drone defense helped secure the massive JUNO Awards crowd

When tens of thousands of fans gather under one roof, security doesn’t just stop at the doors; it extends to the skies above. That was exactly the case at Canada’s biggest night in music, the JUNO Awards, where a quiet but critical layer of anti-drone protection was keeping watch overhead. more…...

Counter-drone technology is becoming standard security infrastructure for large-scale public events, representing a growing commercial application beyond traditional airspace management.

See DroneDJ →

Aerospace Apr 6, 2026

The moon base has a hardware plan. It needs a software strategy, too.

Early in my career at SpaceX, I was the only person dedicated to training the mission control team for Dragon. For three years, I built the training for the operations […]...

Lunar infrastructure development risks repeating Apollo-era mistakes by prioritizing physical construction while underinvesting in mission control software and operator training systems.

Commercial Apr 6, 2026

Press Release: Newly rebranded Aeroplay to unveil Nucleus OS

Aeroplay Entertainment, a leading Content Service Provider (CSP) in the aviation industry, is entering an exciting new chapter. The company is rebranding under is Passenger Experience Partner vision to deliver more integrated and passenger-centric inflight solutions. This transformation is...

Aeroplay's pivot toward integrated passenger experience solutions signals consolidation pressures in aviation software, where airlines increasingly demand unified platforms rather than point solutions.
